notes: Initial batch covers Meskerem 01 through Meskerem 07.
Gregorian dates are month/day hints for the common September alignment and need year-aware conversion for leap-year display.
Summaries are short UI summaries derived from the Synaxarium entries; full Synaxarium text is not duplicated here.

gregorianDate.note: Approximate common Gregorian alignment; confirm with year-aware Ethiopian calendar conversion.

days.title: Meskerem 01
days.seasonNote: Beginning of the Ethiopian year; the Synaxarium calls the day holy and blessed and connects it with renewal.
days.summary: The Synaxarium opens the year with renewal, Job's healing at the Tekaze, the Apostle Bartholomew, Abba Melyos, Abba Melki, and the festival of Raguel the angel.

commemorations.title: Beginning of the Ethiopian year

expandedContent.whyCelebrated: The Synaxarium appoints this remembrance for Meskerem 01 (September 11).
whatHappened: Beginning of the Ethiopian year
This entry is classified in the calendar data as liturgical-year / new-year.
A fuller narrative should be reviewed against the Synaxarium PDF before adding more detail.
expandedContent.significance: This remembrance helps the faithful keep the Church calendar with prayer, gratitude, and attention to the witness of the saints and holy events.

days.shortDescription: The Synaxarium commemorates Abba Dioscoros, Archbishop of Alexandria, Abba Sawiros of Gabla, several martyrs, Fasiladas and his companions, and the birth of Hanna the mother of our Lady.

expandedContent.whyCelebrated: The Synaxarium commemorates Abba Dioscoros, Archbishop of Alexandria, Abba Sawiros of Gabla, several martyrs, Fasiladas and his companions, and the birth of Hanna the mother of our Lady.
whatHappened: Abba Dioscoros, Archbishop of Alexandria
Abba Sawiros, Bishop of Gabla
Agathon, Peter, John, Ammon, Ammona, and their mother Rapika
Fasiladas and two thousand of his companions
Das, Gagra, Nawla Bishop of Antioch, and Peter the monk
expandedContent.significance: This day helps the faithful keep the Church calendar with attention to the saints, feasts, holy events, and the mercy of God remembered in the Synaxarium.
